name: debugging
description: Comprehensive debugging specialist for errors, test failures, log analysis,
capabilities: Analyzes error messages and stack traces across multiple programming languages
Parses log files to extract errors, exceptions, and patterns
Identifies root causes through systematic investigation workflows
Detects common error patterns including memory leaks, race conditions, and timeouts
Provides guidance for reproducing and fixing bugs
author: Joseph OBrien
securitySummary: This is a documentation-focused debugging skill. The static analyzer flagged 134 findings, but 100% are false positives. All flagged patterns are examples in markdown documentation showing common debugging commands and anti-patterns, not actual executable code. The only executable component is a 125-line Python log parser script that safely reads user-specified files and outputs JSON analysis. No network access, no credential handling, no arbitrary code execution.
valueStatement: Debugging complex errors and analyzing logs takes significant time and expertise. This skill provides structured workflows for error analysis, log parsing, stack trace investigation, and root cause identification across multiple languages and platforms.
userTitle: Debug code errors and analyze logs
limitations: Cannot access external systems or databases directly
Cannot execute code or run tests on user systems
Cannot modify production systems or deploy fixes
Analysis is based on provided error information and log content

useCases.title: Fix production bugs
useCases.description: Analyze error logs and stack traces to identify root causes and implement fixes

useCases.title: Debug test failures
useCases.description: Investigate why tests are failing and identify the underlying code issues

useCases.title: Investigate incidents
useCases.description: Correlate errors across services and conduct postmortem analysis

promptTemplates.title: Analyze error message
promptTemplates.prompt: Debug this error: [paste error message and stack trace]

promptTemplates.title: Parse log file
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze the error logs in [file path] and identify the root cause

promptTemplates.title: Detect patterns
promptTemplates.prompt: Find patterns in these error logs from the past 24 hours

promptTemplates.title: Fix code issue
promptTemplates.prompt: Fix the null reference error at [file:line] with minimal changes

bestPractices: Start with symptoms before proposing solutions
Test hypotheses systematically rather than guessing
Implement minimal fixes that address root causes
antiPatterns: Applying fixes without understanding root causes
Ignoring error patterns across multiple incidents
Making large changes when a small fix suffices

faq.question: Which programming languages are supported?
faq.answer: JavaScript, Node.js, Python, Java, and other languages with standard stack trace formats.

faq.question: What log formats can be parsed?
faq.answer: Plain text logs, JSON logs, and common log formats with timestamps and error levels.

faq.question: How does this integrate with my IDE?
faq.answer: The skill works with any text-based error information. Copy-paste errors from your terminal or IDE.

faq.question: Is my log data processed externally?
faq.answer: No. All analysis happens locally. The Python script reads files and outputs JSON locally.

faq.question: Why is my debugging not finding the issue?
faq.answer: Ensure you provide the full error message, stack trace, and relevant log context. Some issues require additional logging.

faq.question: How is this different from standard error messages?
faq.answer: This skill provides structured analysis workflows, pattern detection, and systematic root cause identification.
